issues.title: [EPIC] Site redesign 2026-07: polish/UI layer overhaul from KK teardown
issues.body:
## Source

KK full-site teardown, 2026-07-17. Verdict: the underlying site is fine, the polish and UI layer is incohesive and does not feel like him. The strong content exists but is hidden or badly surfaced.

## Design direction (applies to every child issue)

**Keep:** the KK logo top left, the rainbow metallic gradient, the main headline typeface.

**Principles:**
- Surface the good stuff. Photos, talks, clients, testimonials, and writing are the assets; layouts must show them, not bury them.
- Human voice, not AI voice. No 'rooms', no 'dispatches', no 'field notes', no mouthful taglines. KK voice per the kk-voice corpus. Never use em dashes in site copy.
- Interactivity and hover states everywhere they earn their keep. Static black-on-white blocks are the enemy.
- Cohesion over novelty. One stylesheet hierarchy, consistent backgrounds, consistent column widths.

## Waves

- **Wave 1 (swarm now):** quick wins and bug fixes. Marquee move, nav change, hero image bug, pill/overlay CSS bug, compact writing cards.
- **Wave 2 (draft-first):** section and page redesigns. Agents produce proposals, mockups, and staged drafts; nothing publishes without KK review.
- **Decisions:** stylesheet rebuild strategy and primary CTA need a KK call before implementation.

## Child issues

Checklist added after batch creation with real issue numbers.

## Epic done when

- [ ] Every child issue closed or explicitly parked by KK
- [ ] KK looks at the homepage and every main-nav page and says it feels like him

issues.title: [NAV] Remove Podcasts from main nav; relocate to footer
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> Podcasts should come out of the main nav. My podcast is old. It's not where I want to place my focus right now, but you can put it in the footer.

## Scope

- Remove Podcasts from the primary navigation menu
- Add a Podcasts link to the footer (coordinates with the footer bento issue, but do not wait for it; a plain footer link now is fine)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Podcasts absent from the main nav on desktop and mobile menus
- [ ] Podcasts link present and working in the footer
- [ ] No other nav items reordered or removed

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Logged-out render of `/` at 375, 768, 1440 widths: no Podcasts in header nav, link present in footer, footer link returns 200
- [ ] Check at least 2 inner pages to confirm the nav change is global
- [ ] No console errors introduced

## Safety

- Snapshot the menu state before editing (wp-live-edit flow, snapshot under backup/)
- Pagely: REST edits do not auto-purge; purge and verify logged-out

issues.title: [HOME] Move marquee board from top of homepage to bottom
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> It looks broken and static. I don't know why it's there. Until we figure out what it's supposed to do, we should just put it at the bottom of this page.

## Scope

- Relocate the marquee board block from its current top-of-page position to the bottom of the homepage, above the footer
- Do not restyle or fix it here; improvement is tracked in the marquee research issue

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Homepage opens with the hero, not the marquee
- [ ] Marquee renders at the bottom, above the footer, still functional to its current standard
- [ ] No layout breakage in adjacent sections

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Logged-out render at 375, 768, 1440: section order verified by screenshot
- [ ] 3 cache-cold loads: no console errors from the marquee in its new position
- [ ] Lighthouse quick pass: no LCP regression from the reorder

## Safety

- Snapshot homepage content before editing (backup/ + wp-live-edit flow)
- Purge Pagely cache and verify logged-out

issues.title: [MARQUEE] Research spike: what should the marquee board actually do
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> It looks like a concept that was half-baked and not rolled out. You can pull out the roadmap there and do some research and come up with ways to make it more awesome.

## Scope

Draft-only research. Produce a proposal doc at `docs/proposals/marquee-board-2026-07.md` covering:

- What the board is today (plugin code in `plugins/`, `make marquee-package`, `marquee-weekly.yml` workflow, `origin/marquee/weekly-proposals` branch)
- 3 to 5 concrete concepts for what it could be (live ticker of talks/posts/events, now-playing style status, community signal board, etc), each with effort estimate and data source
- A recommendation with rationale

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Proposal doc exists with the sections above
- [ ] Each concept names its data source and refresh mechanism
- [ ] Recommendation is decision-ready for KK

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Doc reviewed against the existing marquee plugin code, not written from imagination; cites actual file paths
- [ ] No live-site changes of any kind in this issue

## Safety

- Read-only against the live site; writes only to docs/

issues.title: [BUG] Homepage hero image does not load correctly
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> The next hero section is kind of okay, except the image doesn't load right.

## Scope

- Reproduce and root-cause the hero image failure (candidates: lazyload/reveal race, srcset/sizes mismatch, hotlinked asset, cache variant)
- Fix in theme source (`theme/`) or page content as the root cause dictates

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Root cause written up in the issue before the fix lands
- [ ] Hero image renders correctly on cold and warm cache
- [ ] Image served from the kriskrug.co media library, not hotlinked

## Evals / verification

- [ ] 5 consecutive cache-cold logged-out loads of `/`: hero image visible and correctly sized every time
- [ ] Verified at 375, 768, 1440 and with JS disabled (image must still appear or degrade gracefully)
- [ ] Image request returns 200 with correct srcset candidates; no 4xx in the network log
- [ ] Descriptive alt text present

## Safety

- Theme changes go through the Aurora package flow (`make aurora-package`), not ad-hoc live edits
- Snapshot before any content-side change

issues.title: [BUG] Broken pill styles and stylesheet overlay conflicts (Ecosystem, Festival, Creative Labs)
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> The graphical parts, the pills are fucked up... you've done some overlays and there's something fucked up with the style sheet. Those pill sections are broken.

Same symptom appears in at least three homepage sections, so treat it as one root cause until proven otherwise.

## Scope

- Root-cause the pill/overlay breakage across Ecosystem, Festival, and Creative Labs sections
- Fix the shared CSS; do not add per-section !important patches
- Document findings; feed structural conclusions into the stylesheet rebuild decision issue and #256 (CSS dead-code audit)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Written root-cause diagnosis in the issue
- [ ] Pills render as designed in all three sections
- [ ] No overlay/stacking-context artifacts remain
- [ ] Fix is a shared-layer change, not three local patches

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Before/after screenshots of all three sections at 375, 768, 1440, logged out
- [ ] grep the diff: no new `!important` declarations
- [ ] Hover and focus states on pills work with keyboard and mouse
- [ ] No horizontal overflow introduced at 375

## Safety

- Theme changes via `make aurora-package` deploy flow with rollback ref

issues.title: [PAGE] Writing page: compact cards, surface more content per screen
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> We need to make all those little cards smaller or more compact. There's so much in my world that you have to scroll around to go find, and I really want to get more of it up onto the pages.

## Scope

- Redesign the post cards on the Writing archive to a denser layout (smaller cards, tighter grid, thumbnails retained)
- Keep it scannable; density must not become clutter

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] At 1440 width, at least 2x as many posts visible above the fold as before
- [ ] Cards keep thumbnail, title, date; no bare text lists
- [ ] Mobile layout remains single-column and comfortable

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Before/after screenshots at 375, 768, 1440 with visible-post count annotated
- [ ] No cumulative layout shift from image loading (explicit dimensions on thumbnails)
- [ ] Pagination or load-more still works logged out

## Safety

- Theme change via Aurora package flow with rollback ref

issues.title: [COPY] Hero tagline: replace 'Author judgment for generative everything'
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> 'Author judgment for generative everything' is kind of weird. I really don't know what author judgment is. Make sure the typography and the design of that hero is really fucking good.

Also from the newsletter section: > 'Author judgment, human agency, and the camera trained habit of paying attention' is a real mouthful.

## Scope

- Draft 5 candidate hero lines in KK voice, grounded in the kk-voice corpus and the Dark Crystal voice direction
- Hunt the phrase 'author judgment' site-wide and list every occurrence for replacement
- Present options to KK; implement the winner after he picks

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] 5 options drafted, each one sentence, no em dashes, no AI tropes
- [ ] Site-wide occurrence list of 'author judgment' produced
- [ ] KK picks; chosen line deployed to the hero

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Run the voice-slop-audit against the 5 candidates; all pass
- [ ] After deploy: logged-out render shows the new line; `grep -ci 'author judgment'` on rendered homepage HTML returns 0
- [ ] Hero typography reviewed at 375 and 1440 (no orphan words, no overflow)

## Safety

- Draft-first. Nothing publishes until KK approves the line. Snapshot before the content edit.

issues.title: [HOME] 'Join BC / Future Proof' section: rewrite copy, fix alignment, drop cap, and hover states
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> The alignment is off. 'Rooms that need clarity, courage and a next useful step' screams AI. I just don't like the way I talk about myself here. It's not real. The initial drop cap looks ugly, the colors are rudimentary, there's no hovers or interactivity. I do like the main headline typeface.

## Scope

- Rewrite the section copy in KK voice: no 'rooms', no self-description that sounds generated. Draft 2 to 3 options for KK
- Fix alignment between the Join BC and Future Proof columns
- Remove or redesign the drop cap
- Add hover/interaction states to the cards and links
- Keep the headline typeface

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Copy options drafted and KK has picked one
- [ ] The word 'rooms' gone from the section
- [ ] Columns align on a shared grid at desktop widths
- [ ] Drop cap removed or intentionally redesigned
- [ ] All interactive elements have hover and focus states

## Evals / verification

- [ ] voice-slop-audit passes on the chosen copy; zero em dashes
- [ ] `grep -ci 'rooms'` on the rendered section HTML returns 0
- [ ] Screenshots at 375, 768, 1440 confirm alignment
- [ ] Keyboard tab-through reaches every interactive element with a visible focus state

## Safety

- Copy is draft-first behind KK approval; style fixes ship via Aurora package flow

issues.title: [HOME] Creative Labs section redesign: layout, image crops, clarity
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> These sections underneath are awesome but the awesomeness isn't clear from the way you have it laid out. Those pill sections are broken. The images you've chosen are cropped wrong and don't look good. In general it's just not very descriptive of who I am or what I'm about. It's very cryptic.

## Scope

- Redesign the Creative Labs section layout so each lab reads clearly: what it is, why it matters, where to go
- Re-select or re-crop images (art-direct the crops; faces and subjects framed properly)
- Pill fixes land via the shared pill/overlay bug issue; this issue consumes that fix
- Replace cryptic labels with plain descriptions in KK voice

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Design proposal (mockup or staged draft) approved by KK before live deploy
- [ ] Every lab card has a clear title, one-line plain-language description, and a working link
- [ ] Images correctly cropped at all breakpoints (no cut-off heads, no awkward crops)
- [ ] Section passes the 5-second test: a stranger can say what Creative Labs is

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Screenshots at 375, 768, 1440 reviewed against the approved mockup
- [ ] All card links return 200 logged out
- [ ] Images served from media library with alt text; no hotlinks
- [ ] voice-slop-audit on new card copy passes

## Safety

- Draft/mockup first, KK approval gate, then Aurora package deploy with rollback ref

issues.title: [HOME] Bring back the client logo soup: monochromatic and interactive
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> I want to bring in the logo soup. I want them monochromatic and interactive somehow. I have the drop-dead best clients in the world and their logos are showstoppers. Peter and I had done logo work around this with testimonials for our AI upgrade program; you can draw from that work.

## Scope

- Locate the prior logo + testimonial work from the AI upgrade program with Peter (check content/source-packs/, kk-kb, and Notion; list what was found before building)
- Build a monochromatic client logo band for the homepage (CSS filter or mono SVGs)
- Interactive: hover restores color, or reveals a one-line testimonial/engagement note
- Logos load from the kriskrug.co media library

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Source inventory of the Peter-era logo/testimonial assets documented in the issue
- [ ] Logo band ships with at least 8 client logos, monochrome at rest
- [ ] Hover/focus interaction works with mouse and keyboard
- [ ] KK approves the client list before it goes live (which clients appear is his call)

## Evals / verification

- [ ] All logo assets return 200 from the media library, correct aspect ratios, alt text = client name
- [ ] Monochrome treatment consistent across all logos (no one logo in color at rest)
- [ ] Screenshots at 375, 768, 1440; logos wrap gracefully on mobile
- [ ] No layout shift when hover state activates

## Safety

- Client list is a KK approval gate. Snapshot homepage before insert; Aurora package flow for theme changes.

issues.title: [HOME] Speaking stages section: ground-up redesign with links, visuals, interactivity
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> All the good shit here is lost. It's just black on white, high contrast, no links, no interactivity. It sucks. All the good work is hidden. Completely redesign this section, think it from the ground up with an art director.

## Scope

- Treat as an art-direction project, not a patch: propose 2 to 3 distinct visual concepts (stage photos as the backbone, venue/event logos, linked talk titles)
- Every stage/event mentioned links somewhere real (talk video, event page, or writeup)
- Bring in photography; KK has extensive stage photos

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] 2 to 3 concept mockups presented; KK picks a direction
- [ ] Final section is image-led, not text-on-white
- [ ] Every listed engagement has a working link
- [ ] Hover/focus states on all interactive elements

## Evals / verification

- [ ] All links return 200 logged out
- [ ] Images from media library, alt text, no hotlinks, correct crops at 375/768/1440
- [ ] Contrast still passes WCAG AA where text sits on imagery
- [ ] Before/after screenshots attached to the PR

## Safety

- Concept approval gate before build; Aurora package deploy with rollback ref

issues.title: [HOME] 'What People Say' redesign + network diagram spike
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> I've got tons of quotes and testimonials about me, about my talks, about my convening. We should really make this a rad section. This is where we should build a network diagram that somehow actually shows clusters. I've got a badass network and everybody knows it's one of the most valuable assets.

## Scope

Two lanes in one issue:

1. **Testimonials redesign:** gather the strongest quotes (kk-kb testimonial archives, AI upgrade program work, event feedback), curate by theme (talks, convening, programs), design a section that rotates or clusters them with attribution and faces where permitted
2. **Network diagram spike:** prototype an interactive network/cluster visualization of KK's network as a standalone HTML prototype first (not live), for KK to react to

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Curated testimonial set (with sources) documented for KK sign-off; private quotes need explicit permission before public use
- [ ] Redesigned section approved and live
- [ ] Network diagram prototype delivered as a shareable preview; go/no-go decision from KK before any live embed
- [ ] Placeholder 'fresh proof belongs here before launch' inside-baseball text removed

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Every published quote traceable to a real source; no invented or paraphrased-beyond-recognition quotes
- [ ] `grep -ci 'fresh proof'` on rendered homepage returns 0
- [ ] Prototype renders and is interactive in a plain browser with no external CDN dependencies
- [ ] Screenshots at 375, 768, 1440

## Safety

- Testimonial permission gate is hard: nothing publishes without KK confirming each attributed quote is cleared

issues.title: [HOME] Newsletter section: rename, kill the dispatch/field-notes cliche, add thumbnails, one clear CTA
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> I don't like calling it field notes. It says 'field notes' twice and 'dispatches' three times, which makes it cliche and stupid. There's no graphics or thumbnails bubbling up. You're just trying to get people to sign up for the newsletter and give me your emails; think about how to say that. Bring in Dark Crystal Kris Krug voice.

## Scope

- Rename the section (plain: newsletter / writing, KK to pick from drafted options)
- Rewrite copy in KK voice; the CTA is honest and direct about signing up
- Surface recent post thumbnails/graphics in the section
- One clear signup form or button, working end to end

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Copy options drafted, voice-audited, KK picks
- [ ] Rendered section contains at most one instance each of 'field notes' and 'dispatch' (target: zero)
- [ ] Recent posts appear with thumbnails
- [ ] Signup flow verified with a test address end to end

## Evals / verification

- [ ] `grep -c` on rendered section HTML: 'field notes' <= 1, 'dispatch' <= 1, both ideally 0
- [ ] voice-slop-audit passes; zero em dashes
- [ ] Test signup lands in the list (verify in the newsletter platform, then remove the test address)
- [ ] Screenshots at 375, 768, 1440

## Safety

- Copy behind KK approval; snapshot before content edit; purge Pagely and verify logged out

issues.title: [HOME] Footer redesign: integrated bento box
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> We've got a sub navigation hidden down here with all the projects and the utility. It's cool. The whole footer section needs a rethink about how it goes together into an integrated beautiful bento box.

## Scope

- Redesign the footer as a cohesive bento layout: projects sub-nav, utilities, newsletter signup, social, Podcasts link (arriving from the nav issue), contact
- Keep everything currently in the footer; nothing gets dropped without KK's say

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Mockup approved by KK before build
- [ ] All existing footer links preserved and working
- [ ] Podcasts link present
- [ ] Bento layout holds at mobile widths (stacks cleanly, no orphan boxes)

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Link inventory before vs after: zero lost destinations, all return 200 logged out
- [ ] Screenshots at 375, 768, 1440
- [ ] Keyboard navigation reaches every footer link with visible focus
- [ ] Verified on at least 3 page types (home, post, inner page) since the footer is global

## Safety

- Mockup approval gate; Aurora package deploy with rollback ref

issues.title: [PAGE] About page: unify backgrounds, column widths, and kill the double 'public trail'
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> The columns are different widths. 'Public trail' is a weird way to talk about it, and it says it twice. Text is on black, boxes on gray, then a navy background, then another gray. The alignment's lost.

## Scope

- Normalize the About page to one background system and one column grid
- Rewrite or remove the 'public trail' framing (draft alternatives for KK)
- Fix alignment throughout
- Note: About page CSS lives in an inline style block in the page content, edited via WP REST, not the theme

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] One consistent background treatment (or an intentional, documented palette of at most 2)
- [ ] All content columns share a grid; widths consistent section to section
- [ ] 'Public trail' appears at most once, in approved copy, or not at all

## Evals / verification

- [ ] `grep -ci 'public trail'` on rendered /about/ HTML <= 1
- [ ] Full-page screenshots at 375, 768, 1440 reviewed for background and alignment consistency
- [ ] Text contrast passes WCAG AA on every background used
- [ ] Existing About content (roster, Beastie Boys cards, galleries) intact after the edit

## Safety

- Snapshot page 1208 content before editing (backup/); purge Pagely and verify logged out

issues.title: [PAGE] Speaking page: multimedia rebuild that sells keynotes
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> My speaking section is not selling any keynotes. It's way below the fold before you get to any actual photos of me on stages. There's no videos. I've got great talks online, great photos online. This should be a multimedia extravaganza that makes people want to hire me.

## Scope

- Rebuild the Speaking page media-first: stage photo or talk video above the fold
- Inventory KK's best talk videos and stage photography; curate the top set for the page
- Clear booking path (coordinates with the CTA decision issue)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Media inventory (videos + photos with sources) documented for KK curation
- [ ] At 1440 and 390, a stage photo or video is visible without scrolling
- [ ] At least 2 embedded or linked talk videos on the page
- [ ] Booking CTA present above the fold and at page end

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Screenshot at 1440 and 390: media above fold confirmed
- [ ] Video embeds lazy-load (no autoplay, no LCP blowout); Lighthouse LCP within 25% of previous page baseline
- [ ] All media and links 200 logged out; images from media library with alt text
- [ ] 5-second test: a stranger says 'this person speaks on stages and I can book them'

## Safety

- Curation approval from KK before publish; snapshot page before edits

issues.title: [PAGE] Services page: rethink language, layout, and scroll depth
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> The language is weak and the boxes are weak. There's a lot of scroll. The stylesheet's fucked up. This page needs a rethinking from the ground up.

## Scope

- Rewrite services copy in KK voice: concrete offers, plain language, no filler (drafts for KK approval)
- Redesign the layout to cut scroll depth substantially (denser cards, tabs, or merged sections)
- Fix the page's stylesheet issues (coordinate with the stylesheet rebuild decision; do not double-build)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Copy drafted, voice-audited, approved by KK
- [ ] Total page height reduced by at least a third at 1440 without losing offers
- [ ] Every service block states what it is, who it's for, and what to do next

## Evals / verification

- [ ] voice-slop-audit passes; zero em dashes
- [ ] Before/after page-height measurement at 1440 recorded in the PR
- [ ] Screenshots at 375, 768, 1440
- [ ] All CTAs and links 200 logged out

## Safety

- Copy approval gate; snapshot before edit; purge and verify logged out

issues.title: [PAGE] Contact page: add KK's face, real design, clear newsletter language
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> Contact page needs some love. It should have my face on it. It should be well designed. 'Dispatch' is a weird way of linking to my newsletter. I just want a newsletter signup somewhere.

## Scope

- Add a strong KK portrait to the contact page
- Redesign the page layout (form + photo + short human intro in KK voice)
- Replace 'Dispatch' newsletter language with plain 'newsletter' framing and a working signup

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Portrait on the page, served from media library, approved by KK
- [ ] Contact form still works end to end (test submission received at the configured recipient)
- [ ] 'Dispatch' gone; newsletter signup or link present with plain language

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Test form submission arrives (then clean up the test entry)
- [ ] `grep -ci 'dispatch'` on rendered contact page returns 0
- [ ] Screenshots at 375, 768, 1440
- [ ] Portrait has alt text and loads 200 logged out

## Safety

- Contact form routing must not change (recipient stays as configured); snapshot before edit

issues.title: [DECISION] Primary CTA: 'Book a keynote' is too narrow
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> 'Book a keynote' is weird. I'm not sure everyone is coming here to book keynotes, so we need to change our call to action.

## Scope

Decision prep, then implementation after KK picks.

- Map who actually lands on kriskrug.co (speaking bookers, program/consulting clients, community members, press, newsletter readers)
- Draft 3 to 5 CTA options in KK voice, each with rationale and where it points
- Consider a split CTA (primary + secondary) if one label cannot cover the audience

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Audience map + CTA options presented to KK
- [ ] KK picks; new CTA deployed in header and anywhere 'Book a keynote' appears
- [ ] Destination page for the CTA actually serves that intent

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Site-wide inventory of every 'Book a keynote' instance before the change; all updated after
- [ ] voice-slop-audit on options; zero em dashes
- [ ] CTA destination returns 200 and matches the promise of the label

## Safety

- Blocked on KK's pick; drafting and inventory are swarmable now

issues.title: [DECISION] Stylesheet hierarchy: rebuild from the ground up vs incremental repair
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> We need to rethink the stylesheet hierarchy from the beginning. Maybe we just throw them away and rebuild modern new ones from the ground up.

Evidence across the teardown: pill/overlay conflicts on three homepage sections, About page background and grid chaos, Services page styling, missing hover/focus states site-wide.

## Scope

Decision prep first:

- Inventory every CSS surface: theme stylesheets in `theme/`, inline style blocks in page content (About page pattern), Code Snippets CSS (KK Asset Diet), Jetpack Boost critical CSS
- Quantify the mess: dead rules, specificity wars, !important count, duplicated tokens (builds on #256)
- Present two costed paths to KK: (A) ground-up token-based rebuild, (B) staged repair of the existing hierarchy
- After KK picks, this issue becomes the implementation epic for that path

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Complete CSS surface inventory with line counts and load order documented
- [ ] Recommendation memo: path A vs B with effort, risk, and rollback story for each
- [ ] KK decision recorded; implementation plan issued as follow-up tasks

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Inventory cross-checked against live rendered pages (what actually loads, logged out), not just repo contents
- [ ] Numbers in the memo are measured, not estimated (grep counts, file sizes, coverage data)
- [ ] No live changes land under this issue before the decision

## Safety

- Read-only until KK decides. The rebuild, if chosen, ships behind visual-regression screenshots of every key page.

issues.title: [QA] Site-wide hover, focus, and interactivity pass
issues.body:
## Context (KK teardown 2026-07-17)

> There's no hovers or interactivity on any of that shit. Hovers, interactivity, and consistent styling are largely absent across every page.

The section redesign issues each add interaction states locally; this issue is the site-wide sweep and the eval that keeps it true.

## Scope

- Audit every interactive element on the main-nav pages (links, cards, buttons, pills, form fields) for hover and focus states
- Fix gaps with shared styles, not per-element patches
- Runs after the wave-2 section redesigns land; audit can start now to produce the gap list

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Gap inventory: page-by-page list of interactive elements missing hover or focus states
- [ ] Shared interaction styles cover links, buttons, cards, and pills
- [ ] Every main-nav page passes a keyboard tab-through with visible focus on all stops

## Evals / verification

- [ ] Scripted check where possible: computed styles for :hover/:focus-visible differ from rest state on sampled elements per page
- [ ] Manual keyboard tab-through recorded for /, /about/, /speaking/, services, writing, contact
- [ ] No focus outlines suppressed without an equal-or-better replacement

## Safety

- Theme changes via Aurora package flow; coordinate with the stylesheet decision to avoid double work
